Chief Complaint:
LLL spiculated nodule seen on CT
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Right Heart Catheterization (___)
___ guided lung biopsy (___)

History of Present Illness:
___ who underwent ECD kidney transplantation in ___, 
allograft nephropathy with proteinuria and creatinine between 
1.6 and 2 mg/dL, BK viruria without viremia, HTN, HLD who 
presented to ___ after imaging finding of LLL mass. Pt had 
previously tolerated Sirolimus and leflunomide w/o issue, but 
was switched to tacro from Sirolimus on ___ persistent 
proteinuria and ___ edema. In ___ edema and SOB, CXR showed 
LLL mass which was confirmed on CT-chest a few days later. 

Pt states that in the last few months he has had increasing SOB 
and ___ edema. Pt with decreased exercise tolerance. Denies 
hemoptysis, F/C/NS, chest pain. No new travel. No orthopnea. 
 
In the ED, initial vitals were: 98.2 73 84/62 16 100% RA 
Exam notable for ___ swelling
Labs notable for Cr 2.5, BUN 46, BNP 7899, H&H 10.5/33.7, INR 
1.1 
Imaging notable for large nodule in LLL on NCCT chest
Patient was given 1L NS in the ED
Patient was seen by Renal Transplant who recommended admission 
for ___ lung nodule. Once TB r/o per Pulm, will consider 
IP lung biopsy.
Vitals notable for hypotension, was triggered and given 1L NS, 
BPs recovered
 
On the floor, pt was stable with mild occasional cough

in ___, creatinine is overall stable between 1.6 and 2

BK viruria  
Hypertension.  
Dyslipidemia.  
Hepatitis B core antibody positivity, he had hepatitis Bs

Eczema
AVG LUE
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
No hx of lung Dz

Hospital Course:
This is a ___ who underwent ECD kidney transplantation in ___, BK viruria without viremia, HTN, HLD who presented to 
___ after imaging finding of LLL mass in the setting of 
increasing ___ edema and SOB. Initial concern for TB, which was 
r/o, ___ guided biopsy showed SCC. Thoracic surgery/Oncology 
followed pt after Dx was made. Pt's TTE found severe Pulm HTN 
with Cor Pulmonale and mod-severe 3+ Tricuspid Regurg. Rt heart 
cath confirmed Pulm HTN, unclear etiology, no PE seen on CT-A. 
Was started on Sildanifil and will f/u with Pulm outpatient. Pt 
w/ subtherapeutic tacolimus level on admission and tacrolimus 
increased to 6 mg BID with level to be checked on ___. Brain 
MRI negative for metastasis and patient with have PET on 
___. Pt w/ ___ during admission, first secondary to 
aggressive diuresis, and later likely secondary to hypovolemia 
(FeNa<1%), stable at 2.1 on discharge.

ACTIVE ISSUES:
==============
#Squamous Cell Carcinoma: Pt initially presented with LLL mass 
of unclear etiology. He was afebrile w/o significant cough or 
chills, normal  He had a history of latent TB, s/p treatment w/ 
Rifampin and INH for 4 months. He underwent an extensive 
infectious work up which was negative for TB and other 
infections (nocardia, fungal, viral), as well as neg for PTLD, 
and was eventually biopsied by interventional radiology 
(originally planned by IP but deferred with discovery of severe 
Pulm HTN). Touch prep of the specimen was consistent with ___ 
and core patholo

Chief Complaint:
Hypotension
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:

___ - ___: Completed XRT as scheduled ___ fractions on

History of Present Illness:
___ with pHTN, ESRD s/p DCDRT in ___ c/b BK viruria and 
allograft nephropathy, who was recently diagnosed with Stage 3a 
squamous cell Lung CA (___) s/p 3 doses of taxol/carboplatin 
with radiation, admitted from clinic for anorexia, fatigue, 
weight loss, and hypotension.

Mr. ___ was recently diagnosed with SCC of the Lung, stage 3a 
per PET CT in ___. He was started on chemo-rads with 
taxol/carboplatin (s/p 3 doses) and was presenting to clinic 
today for his fourth weekly dose. Per his oncologist, he has 
been doing well with his therapy thus far without any 
significant side effects either clinically or lab-wise.

Per patient, about 2 weeks prior to clinic visit and admission, 
he noticed acute-onset anorexia, fatigue, and malaise. He denies 
any changes in medications or otherwise in his health. During 
this same time period, he has had mild nausea with 2 episodes of 
NBNB vomiting about 3 days PTA, as well as mild odynophagia and 
dysphagia (sensation of food getting stuck at level of 
manubrium) to be both solids and liquids, without any 
hematemesis. He has had weight loss of ~10 pounds over the past 
2 weeks ___ poor PO intake and has had progressive LH and 
dizziness, 
[... truncated ...]

Past Medical History:
PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY: per onc notes
- On ___ he underwent CT-guided biopsy of the mass and the
pathology revealed focally keratizing (p63 and for TTF-1 
negative) squamous cell cancer of the lung. 
-An MRI of the brain on ___ did not show any CNS disease.
-On ___ he underwent a PET-CT that revealed a 5.6 x 3.4 x 
4.2 cm left lower lobe lung mass (SUV max 9.03) and small left 
pleural, non-hemorrhagic effusion. There was a 3mm parafissural 
RUL nodule (unchanged) and scattered micronodules. There was 
fullness in the right hilum without FDG avidity and a 4 mm level 
4L lymph node with low-lev
[... truncated ...]

Hospital Course:
Mr. ___ is a ___ y/o man with ESRD s/p DCDRT in ___ on tacro, 
idiopathic pHTN, and recently diagnosed Stage 3a Squamous Cell 
Lung CA on ___, presenting to clinic with 
fluid-responsive hypotension I/s/o poor PO intake x10 days. 
Found to have moraxella PNA, improved on abx, as well as overall 
FTR likely I/s/o chemo/rad toxicity.

# Failure to Thrive: BMI 16 with associated muscle wasting. The 
patient presented with significant weight loss (nearly 10 pounds 
in 2 weeks) as well as hypotension in clinic likely resulting 
from combination of pneumonia (as below) and overall failure to 
thrive. Since beginning chemo-radiation, he had had anorexia, 
odynophagia (as below), and decreased energy levels. In the 
hospital, his multiple co-morbidities were managed as below and 
for his FTR, he was provided appetite and energy stimulation 
with dronabinol and Ritalin (both continued on discharge) as 
well as aggressive nutritional supplementation. He was offered a 
PEG tube to further support his nutritional intake, which the 
patient declined. Throughout this hospitalization, he continued 
to have significant nausea, vomiting, malaise, pain, and 
coughing and was supported with symptomatic management (as 
detailed below). On discharge to rehab, he was still 
experiencing these side effects of his cancer and 
chemo-radiation treatments, but managed on a stable regimen.
